html:
Rabbit Holes are the home and spawning place for Rabbits. They run back to their holes at dusk, or when they are frightened. When a Rabbit is captured or killed, another will eventually respawn from the hole. A Rabbit Hole can be dug up with a Shovel to force a Rabbit out; however, this will destroy the hole, preventing future Rabbits from spawning there. If a Rabbit is caught outside its hole after dark for any reason, it will fall asleep.

A Rabbit Hole will spawn a new rabbit every 2.5 days. Since a Morsel restores 12.5 hunger and the necessary calorie value per day is 75, this means that having access to 15 Rabbit Holes would be enough to sustain oneself through winter ([75/12.5]*2.5=15). It is also of note that a Rabbit that manages to flee into a Rabbit Hole after e.g a failed hunting attempt will not trigger the 2.5 days respawn timer. Instead, it will occupy the Rabbit Hole and leave it again (if the conditions are met) after a random amount of time between 1 and 3 real-time minutes.

Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants DLC, during Spring, Rabbit Holes will turn into Collapsed Rabbit Holes and won't spawn Rabbits anymore. A few days after Spring has ended, Collapsed Rabbit Holes will go back to normal and start spawning Rabbits again. A Collapsed Rabbit Hole can be dug up as well to force a Rabbit out, this will also destroy the hole forever.

Tips

  • Putting a Trap directly above a Rabbit Hole will ensure a captured Rabbit most of the time.
    • In the base game, multiple traps can be placed after the first trap has caught a Rabbit, to stockpile Rabbits. This is useful during Winter or when one wishes to farm Nightmare Fuel when the Rabbits have turned into Beardlings.

Gallery

  • A winter Rabbit close to its hole.
  • A Collapsed Rabbit Hole in Spring.
  • spider nest next to rabbit hole
  • eyeplant attacking a rabbit off screen
DSTDSRoGSWHam

Don't Starve
Together
Rabbit Hole
Normal
Collapsed (RoG)
Object Info
Renewable?
No
Source
Can be Dug
Savanna
Grasslands
Spawns
Miscellaneous
"rabbithole"
“That must lead to the Kingdom of the Bunnymen.”
–Wilson
“Stupid rabbits. Come out so I can eat you.”
–Willow
“You can not hide forever, jumping meat!”
–Wolfgang
“I'm too big to fall down there.”
–Wendy
“THEY ARE ALL CONNECTED VIA UNDERGROUND TUNNELS”
–WX-78
“It looks like a small animal's burrow.”
–Wickerbottom
“I doubt there are trees down there.”
–Woodie
“There's a world going on under there.”
–Maxwell
“Based on past experience, I can deduce there are rabbits within.”
–Wagstaff
“Shöwtime, rabbits!”
–Wigfrid
“Come out! We just want to be friends.”
–Webber
“It's rabbit-shaped.”
–Walani
“Thump twice if you are fat and juicy.”
–Warly
“Think they got treasures down thar?”
–Woodlegs
“WHEREFORE ART RABBITS HID'DED?”
–Wilba
“Bouncy Squee Hopper home?”
–Wormwood
“Maybe they're part of an underground resistance.”
–Wheeler
“Lots of excavation work around here.”
–Winona
“Hopping wonders live within.”
–Wortox
“Rabbit house.”
–Wurt
“We found its den!”
–Walter
“I'd better watch my step, wouldn't want to fall down a rabbit hole.”
–Wanda
Rabbit Holes are the home and spawning place for Rabbits. They run back to their holes at dusk, or when they are frightened. When a Rabbit is captured or killed, another will eventually respawn from the hole. A Rabbit Hole can be dug up with a Shovel to force a Rabbit out; however, this will destroy the hole, preventing future Rabbits from spawning there. If a Rabbit is caught outside its hole after dark for any reason, it will fall asleep.
A Rabbit Hole will spawn a new rabbit every 2.5 days. Since a Morsel restores 12.5 hunger and the necessary calorie value per day is 75, this means that having access to 15 Rabbit Holes would be enough to sustain oneself through winter ([75/12.5]*2.5=15). It is also of note that a Rabbit that manages to flee into a Rabbit Hole after e.g a failed hunting attempt will not trigger the 2.5 days respawn timer. Instead, it will occupy the Rabbit Hole and leave it again (if the conditions are met) after a random amount of time between 1 and 3 real-time minutes.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
“The Kingdom of the Bunnymen is closed for the season.”
–Wilson, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Stupid rabbits must be stuck down there.”
–Willow, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Is busy time in rabbit home.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“It's closed up. No one is falling down there, now.”
–Wendy, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“UNDERGROUND TUNNEL NETWORK HAS COLLAPSED”
–WX-78, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“I hope the small animal is still all right down there!”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Nothing going in or out.”
–Woodie, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“The entrance has collapsed in on itself.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“I deduce the muddy season has caused this hole to collapse.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“It must be intermissiön för the rabbits.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“I hope they're okay in there.”
–Webber, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“I guess they needed Me Time.”
–Walani, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“What a pity rabbit season has ended.”
–Warly, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Them lil' rabbits ain't comin' out.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“WHEREFORE ART RABBIT HOLE?”
–Wilba, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Where'd they go?”
–Wormwood, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Hope they're relaxing in little mud baths down there.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Guess it wasn't structurally sound.”
–Winona, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“You'll not be hopping through that hole!”
–Wortox, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Anybody home, florp?”
–Wurt, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“Looks like a cave-in.”
–Walter, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
“No more rabbit hole, for now.”
–Wanda, when examining a Collapsed Rabbit Hole.
In the Reign of Giants DLC, during Spring, Rabbit Holes will turn into Collapsed Rabbit Holes and won't spawn Rabbits anymore. A few days after Spring has ended, Collapsed Rabbit Holes will go back to normal and start spawning Rabbits again. A Collapsed Rabbit Hole can be dug up as well to force a Rabbit out, this will also destroy the hole forever.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Putting a Trap directly above a Rabbit Hole will ensure a captured Rabbit most of the time.
In the base game, multiple traps can be placed after the first trap has caught a Rabbit, to stockpile Rabbits. This is useful during Winter or when one wishes to farm Nightmare Fuel when the Rabbits have turned into Beardlings.
Gallery[edit | edit source]
A winter Rabbit close to its hole.
A Collapsed Rabbit Hole in Spring.
